candidates.why: Explicit top-line revenue label; identical values to Net sales and is in candidate list.

variables.notes: Selected the explicit revenue total from candidates. Although 'Net sales' is also a valid top-line label, 'Total Revenue' is preferred here because it is in the candidate list and exactly matches the top-line concept.

candidates.why: Direct input/production cost line and the clearest cost-of-sales proxy.
Operating cost but not typically core cost of goods sold; more like occupancy overhead.

variables.notes: Chose the clearest direct production/service cost line. Excluded 'Cost of premises' as overhead rather than COGS.

candidates.why: Likely operating overhead bucket; candidate and populated.
Explicit SG&A row exists, which per rule should be selected alone when present.
Classic overhead/personnel component, but explicit SG&A row exists so components are not preferred over that row.
Overhead-type line, but explicit SG&A row exists so not needed.
Balance sheet asset line, not income statement expense.
Headcount metric, not expense.

variables.notes: Per SG&A hierarchy, selected only the explicit SG&A row. Did not add other overhead components to avoid double counting. Did not include any R&D row because no dedicated income-statement R&D expense line is present.

candidates.row_label: Amortization of Capitalized Research & Development Expenses - Supplemental
candidates.why: R&D-related but this is amortization, not current-period R&D expense; supplemental and below operating profit area.
Capitalized balance sheet asset, not expense.

candidates.row_label: Research & Development Costs - Accumulated Amortization & Impairment
candidates.why: Balance sheet contra-asset, not current R&D expense.

candidates.row_label: Research & Development Costs - Net - Balancing value
candidates.why: Balancing line, not expense.

variables.notes: No suitable dedicated R&D expense line on the income statement. The only R&D-related line is supplemental amortization of capitalized R&D, which should not be used as XRD.

candidates.why: Direct recurring interest expense on borrowings; strong match.
Correct concept but appears unpopulated/unused relative to granular rows.
Recurring lease interest expense; should be included with other recurring financing costs.
May include recurring financing costs, but could overlap with more specific interest rows and may include non-interest items.
Not a finance cost.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Same concept as right-of-use interest but supplemental/duplicate of the granular lease-interest row.
Equity line, not interest expense.
Equity line, not interest expense.
Liability balance, not expense.
Liability balance, not expense.

variables.notes: Selected granular recurring interest expense components and excluded FX financing gains/losses and net finance totals to avoid non-recurring items and double counting. Excluded 'Other financial expense' because it may overlap and is less clearly pure interest.

candidates.why: Income statement profit attribution line, not balance sheet equity.
Owner/parent equity line that excludes non-controlling interests, ideal for BE.
Includes non-controlling interests, so less preferred when owner equity exists.
Shareholder count, not equity value.
Balancing line, not preferred.
Balancing line, not preferred.

candidates.row_label: Balancing Item - Total Liability and Equity
candidates.why: Balancing line, not equity.
Total liabilities plus equity, not BE.

variables.notes: Preferred owner equity line because it excludes non-controlling interests, consistent with BE + MIB construction.

candidates.why: Income statement attribution line, not balance sheet minority equity.
Explicit balance sheet NCI line; strong match.
Equivalent balance sheet minority interest label; also strong match and may vary by year.
Income statement attribution total, not balance sheet equity.

candidates.row_label: Total Comprehensive Income Attributable to Non-Controlling Interests
candidates.why: Comprehensive income attribution, not minority equity balance.

variables.notes: Used balance sheet non-controlling interest labels as priority list to capture possible label variation across years. BE uses parent equity only, so including MIB separately avoids double counting.
